resources.string: Unlock your Android device bootloader via USB
Connect the target Android phone to this device using a USB-C cable. Enable USB Debugging and OEM Unlocking in Developer Options on the target phone first.
Detecting connected device and verifying ADB/Fastboot communication.
Rebooting target device into Fastboot mode.
Checking if the bootloader can be unlocked on this device.
Sending unlock command. You must confirm on the target device screen.
WARNING: Unlocking the bootloader will wipe all data on the target device. Make sure you have backed up everything important.
OEM Unlocking must be enabled in Developer Options on the target device before proceeding.
This device does not support USB Host (OTG) mode.
USB permission denied. Please reconnect the device.
This device has unlock_ability=0. The manufacturer has disabled bootloader unlocking.
Unlocking the bootloader voids your warranty, wipes all user data, and may affect device security. Only proceed if you understand the consequences and own the target device.
